publishedAbstract: In some instances, it may be necessary to monitor the usage of the noobaa pods. Specifically, noobaa-core0, noobaa-db-pg-0, and noobaa-endpoint and adjust resources as needed IAW the [Performance tuning guide for Multicloud Object Gateway (NooBaa)](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/6719951) or [Standalone NooBaa Multicloud Object Gateway (MCG) Performance Tuning Guide (without StorageCluster CR)- Quay](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7049634) solutions. **Related Articles:** ["504 Gateway Timeout" During NooBaa Bucket Operations- OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF)](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7032646) [Performing Maintenance on a Growing PostgreSQL Database](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7117342) [Investigating and Fixing Duplicates inside NooBaa-DB PostgreSQL DB Causing Backingstore Flapping or Performance Degradation in OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF)](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7110605) [How to Check the Size/Consumption of the PostgreSQL Database in the db-noobaa-db-pg-0 PVC](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7116750)
publishedTitle: How to Monitor Multicloud Object Gateway (MCG)/NooBaa Resources OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF)
